name: Golden Crown Restaurant House Special
recipeIngredient: 0.25 lb (60 to 75 per pound) peeled small shrimp
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil chow mein noodles according to package directions. Drain.
2. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok or very large frying pan. Stir-fry noodles for 3 to 5 minutes, or until crisp and light brown. Remove from wok and set aside.
3. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons oil and garlic. Add bean sprouts, bok choy, carrot, green pepper, celery, water chestnuts, bamboo shoots, mushrooms, and onion to the wok. Stir-fry for several minutes over high heat.
4. Add shrimp, chicken, and barbecued pork. Continue stir-frying several minutes.
5. Add broth and cover with a lid. Cook with lid on for 3 to 4 minutes.
6. Combine cornstarch with cold water. Stir in oyster sauce, sugar, MSG (if used), and salt. Add the sauce and noodles to the wok. Continue cooking just until the sauce thickens about 1 minute. Serve immediately.
7. Makes 6 servings.
SOURCE: From the Golden Crown Restaurant, Beaverton, Oregon. September '91 FOODday, Oregonian Newspaper. Shared by Cate Vanicek. Recipe courtesy of Catherine Vanicek, 07 Mar 93, 21:13:16
